Fixing 'npm WARN config global --global, --local are deprecated. Use --location=global instead'
After updating to Node.js 16.15.1 and npm 8.11.0, I kept seeing "npm WARN config global --global, --local are deprecated. Use --location=global instead". Here's a note on the fix.
For dev work I often use nvm to install other Node.js versions. One day I installed 16.15.1:
language-cmd shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast

nvm install 16.15.1
nvm use 16.15.1
node -v // 16.15.1
npm -v  // 8.11.0
npm WARN config global --global, --local are deprecated. Use --location=global instead
The fix is dead simple — just update npm:
But during that, you might hit "
". That error isn't a Node.js or npm problem — it's an nvm-windows issue. Fix is also simple: download the nvm-windows update package.
error should be gone. Close the terminal, then reopen it as
Then remove the problematic Node.js version and reinstall:

nvm uninstall 16.15.1
nvm install 16.15.1
nvm use 16.15.1
npm WARN config global --global, --local are deprecated. Use --location=global instead
After the update, the warning's gone.
This is probably an npm 8.11.0 issue, so just update to the newer version. If the warning's still there after updating, I'd recommend reinstalling Node.js and restarting the terminal.
